summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering police and fire department staffing, fleet purchases, zoning updates, and community development initiatives. Four new full-time police officers were appointed and two firefighter paramedic hires were authorized. The board approved the purchase of two new Ford police interceptor SUVs and a new collective bargaining agreement with the firefighters union. Discussions included the Combs Trucking legal case, reestablishing a Community Investment Corporation (CIC), the township branding logo, and proposed zoning text amendments.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for January 27 through February 9, 2021
Approved out-of-town travel for Sergeant Dylan West to attend impact weapons instructor training at $230
Approved purchase of two Ford police interceptor SUVs from Lebanon Ford for $66,800
Authorized activation of UAN bank reconciliation opt-in feature for fiscal officer
Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter EMT Logan Weisenberg effective February 5, 2021
Appointed Charlie Saylor as full-time police officer at $65,278.91 effective 2-19-21
Appointed Kyle Hawke as full-time police officer at $50,328.08 effective 2-20-21
Appointed Tyler Smith (contingent on pre-employment screening) as full-time police officer at $50,328.08 effective 2-21-21
Appointed Lewis Carter as full-time police officer at $50,328.08 effective 2-20-21
Authorized hiring of probationary full-time firefighter paramedic effective February 14, 2021
Authorized hiring of probationary full-time firefighter paramedic effective February 29, 2021 to replace retiring Lt. Daryl Roberts
Approved 2021-2023 collective bargaining agreement with Goshen Professional Firefighters Union Local 3932
action_items: Steve Pegram to continue pursuing CIC (Community Investment Corporation) reestablishment and schedule a future presentation
Steve Pegram to do social media posts about the 2021 strategic plan
Steve Pegram to prepare budget comparison documents for strategic plan adoption by March/April
Invite Eric Brown to the February 23rd meeting to finalize branding logo discussion
Steve Pegram/Chad to get estimates on a sign for the fire department using potential Duke Energy foundation funding
Harry Hilbert to present zoning text amendments to Clermont County Planning Commission on February 23
Chad and service department to clean up 1260 Clarewell property
Set up interviews for zoning board applicants and zoning inspector position
Damaged service department truck to be shipped out to a repair shop
key_amounts: $230 for Sergeant West impact weapons instructor training
$66,800 for two Ford police interceptor SUVs from Lebanon Ford
$65,278.91 annual salary for Charlie Saylor (full-time police officer)
$50,328.08 annual salary for Kyle Hawke, Tyler Smith, and Lewis Carter (probationary police officers)
~$40 per legal newspaper notice for property abatement
~$3,000 estimated savings by using township staff for 1260 Clarewell cleanup
$5,000+ for private cleanup on Route 48 property
notable_topics: Snow removal operations and service department truck breakdown
Combs Trucking legal case update - 14-year civil/criminal case with Attorney General's office
Four new full-time police officer appointments
2021-2023 firefighters union collective bargaining agreement approval
Purchase of two new police SUVs
Zoning text amendment regarding accessory structure lot coverage and height limits
Dollar General approved with permit on Route 28
Township branding logo discussion - disagreement on design elements
2021 strategic plan update and adoption timeline
Duke Energy community liaison meeting and potential sign funding
UAN bank reconciliation opt-in feature activation
Resignation of part-time firefighter EMT Logan Weisenberg
attendees: Chad (Service Department, absent due to snow operations)
notes: Trustee Hauserman expressed strong caution about CIC based on past negative experience with a previous CIC that had governance and control issues. Disagreement among trustees on branding logo - Trustee Hodges dislikes the sun/grass graphic elements calling them 'cartoon.' Trustee Hauserman opposed reducing accessory structure height limit from 25 feet to 18 feet as recommended by zoning commission. Executive session held from 7:55 PM to 8:37 PM. February 29, 2021 was mentioned as an effective date for a firefighter hire, but 2021 is not a leap year - likely an error in the transcript. Logan Weisenberg resigned to become a police officer in Fort Myers, Florida.
title: BOT 2.9.2021
